title: Fixing CSS Pseudo-Class Selectors for Dynamic Styling

statement.markdown: A web developer is trying to implement a dynamic styling feature using CSS pseudo-class selectors. However, the logic is incorrect, causing inconsistent styling. Your task is to fix the bug in the provided CSS selector implementation to ensure that the correct styles are applied based on the user interaction.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string representing the CSS selector, a pseudoclass name, and a user interaction event (e.g., hover, focus).
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a string representing the corrected CSS selector with the pseudoclass name and user interaction event.
statement.notes: The CSS selector should correctly apply the styles based on the user interaction event.

examples.explanation: The CSS selector should correctly apply the styles when the user hovers over the nth child of the mth child of a list item.
The CSS selector should correctly apply the styles when the button is focused.

hints.text: Check the order of the pseudoclass name and the user interaction event in the CSS selector.

editorial.title: Editorial for Fixing CSS Pseudo-Class Selectors
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem is to correctly apply the pseudoclass name and the user interaction event in the CSS selector.
editorial.bruteForce: One approach is to manually try different combinations of the pseudoclass name and the user interaction event to see which one works.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the correct syntax for the CSS selector, which is to place the pseudoclass name after the element selector and the user interaction event.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the corrected CSS selector will correctly apply the styles based on the user interaction event.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the problem can be done by trying different inputs and outputs to see which one works.
commonMistakes: Incorrectly placing the pseudoclass name and the user interaction event in the CSS selector.
